name: Prof. Jagadeesh Bayry
field: Immunology, Immunotherapy, Host-Pathogen interaction, Autoimmune diseases, Inflammation, Vaccine
message: Our vision is to combine engineering innovation with biological insights to tackle global health challenges. We foster an environment where curiosity meets rigor to address complex biological questions and redefine the boundaries.
bio:
Prof. Jagadeesh Bayry is a graduate of Veterinary Medicine from Bengaluru (1996) with a specialization of Virology and Immunology from Indian Veterinary Research Institute (1999). He received PhD in Immunology from Sorbonne University, Paris in 2003. Following post-doctoral research at University of Oxford, he joined Institut National de la Santé et de la Recherche Médicale (INSERM, The National Institute of Health and Medical Research), Paris, France in 2006 as a tenured scientist. In 2018, he became Director of Research at INSERM. In 2021, he joined IIT Palakkad as a Professor of Biological Sciences and Engineering. Currently he is a Head of the Department of Biological Sciences & Engineering. He has served as Dean (Student Affairs) at IIT Palakkad from January 2022 to March 2024.

His research is aimed at fundamental Immunology, autoimmunity, inflammation, immunotherapy and host-pathogen interaction. Prof. Bayry has obtained 38 National, European, International, Industry and Research Foundation grants. His h-index is 77 and has authored more than 320 articles in diverse international journals including Nature, New England Journal of Medicine, Science Immunology, Lancet Respiratory Medicine, Blood, Journal of Allergy & Clinical Immunology, Nature Communications, PNAS, Nature Reviews, Journal of Infectious Diseases, mBio, Trends Journals, and many others with >20,000 citations (THE LINK TO FULL LIST OF PUBLICATIONS: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/myncbi/jagadeesh.bayry.1/bibliography/public/). He has many patents, has delivered more than 100 talks in prestigious institutions and conferences, and has guided more than 43 doctoral, post-doctoral and master fellows.

Prof. Bayry served as a member of a commission 'LS6-Immunity, Infection and Immunotherapy' at the European Research Council (ERC) for the consolidated ERC grants (2016 to 2022) and for the qualitative evaluation of completed ERC-funded projects (2022 and 2023). He has also served as an Advisory committee member for the Dutch Research Council. Prof. Bayry has uninterruptedly featured in the list of the Top 2% Scientists of the world (both in career-long data and single recent year data) including the latest one (Version 8, 19th September 2025, https://elsevier.digitalcommonsdata.com/datasets/btchxktzyw/8) released by John P.A. Ioannidis of Stanford University in association with Elsevier. He has been elected as a Fellow of Academy of Translational Medicine Professionals, Austria (FAcadTM, 2016), Fellow of Indian Association of Veterinary Microbiologists, Immunologists and Specialists in Infectious Diseases (FIAVMI, 2024), Fellow of Indian Society of Veterinary Immunology and Biotechnology (FISVIB, 2024), Fellow of Academy for Environment and Life Sciences (FAELS, 2025), Fellow of Society for Applied Biotechnology (FSAB, 2025), Fellow of Indian Academy of Biomedical Sciences (FIABS, 2025), Fellow of Indian College of Allergy, Asthma and Applied Immunology (FICAAI, 2025), Fellow of Biotech Research Society of India (FBRS, 2025), Fellow of Indian Virological Society (FIVS, 2025), Fellow of the Royal Society of Biology, London, UK (FRSB, 2025) and Fellow of National Academy of Biological Sciences (FNABS, 2026).

Prof. Bayry is serving/ has served as an Editor, Associate Editor or Editorial Board member of various journals like Frontiers in Immunology, Cellular & Molecular Immunology, Immunobiology, Discovery Medicine, Scientific Reports, Immunotherapy, Antibodies, PLoS ONE, Frontiers in Molecular Medicine, Virusdisease, and has edited four books from Springer Publisher.

name: Dr. Prasanna Bhat
field: Molecular virology, Host-virus interaction, RNA biology

name: Dr. Prasun Kumar
field: Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, Biophysics, X-ray crystallography, Algorithm development, , T-Cell Receptors, Anitbodies, Structural Bioinformatics
bio: Dr. Kumar specializes in structural bioinformatics and de novo protein design. His research primary characteristic is a synergy between computational and experimental approaches. He has developed several well-used bioinformatics tools for protein structure analyses and also demonstrated hands-on experience in wet lab techniques by solving the first quaternary structure containing exclusively 3₁₀-helices. He also has industry experience as a Senior Bioinformatics Person designing de novo proteins for therapeutic applications. He has also filed a provisional Indian patent titled ‘A method and system for topology-free and anchor-driven protein design’ (Application number: 202641013231).

name: Dr. Sivakumar Vadivel Gnanasundram
field: RNA biology; Molecular cancer; translational control; disease-specific riboregulation
bio: Dr. Sivakumar Vadivel Gnanasundram is an RNA biologist specializing in RNA-mediated gene expression regulation. He completed his Ph.D. at Heidelberg University, Germany, where he uncovered dual roles of the Has1 RNA helicase in ribosome biogenesis and developed a rapid protein depletion system for studying essential proteins in yeast. He then carried out postdoctoral research at INSERM, France, focusing on mRNA translation control under oncogenic stress and RNA-binding proteins. As a Staff Scientist at the Translational Research Centre, Umeå University, Sweden, he led studies on in-cell RNA structures, their roles in stress signalling, and cancer-associated synonymous mutations.

name: Dr. Sushabhan Sadhukhan
bio: Expert in chemical biology and the development of bioactive molecules.

name: Dr. Bibhu Ranjan Sarangi
bio: Focused on mechanobiology and the physical principles of cellular behavior.

name: Dr. Abdul Rasheed P
field: Early stage disease diagnosis, Wearable Bioelectronics, Drug delivery, Biomedical applications of MXenes
bio: Dr Abdul Rasheed P is a researcher in nanobiotechnology focussing on electrochemical biosensing and drug delivery. He obtained his Ph.D. in Nanoscience and Technology from the National Institute of Technology Calicut, followed by postdoctoral research at Korea University, South Korea, and the Qatar Environment and Energy Research Institute, Qatar. His research integrates nanomaterials, electrochemistry, and device engineering, with a strong emphasis on developing low-cost, point-of-care diagnostic technologies. His current work focuses on MXene-based sensor platforms for early detection of neurological and metabolic biomarkers, along with wearable and portable electrochemical devices.
